headline: 3 Reasons to Sell PEP and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Since December 2025, PepsiCo has been in a holding pattern, posting a small loss of 4.1% while floating around $142.12. The stock also fell short of the S&P 500’s 8.9% gain during that period.

headline: PepsiCo (PEP) Stock Could Be 46.9% Below Fair Value as Views Split
summary: PepsiCo (PEP) is back in focus after recent share price moves, with the stock around $142 and short term returns mixed. This raises fresh questions about how investors might view its current valuation. See our latest analysis for PepsiCo. Recent share price moves for PepsiCo look subdued in the short term, with the 30 day share price return down 4.87%, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 14.39% reflects a more supportive longer term picture.
